title: Methyldopa: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text: s. some medications should not be taken with methyldopa. Other medications may cause dosing changes or extra monitoring when taken with methyldopa. Make sure you have discussed any medications you are currently taking or plan to take before starting methyldopa with your doctor and pharmacist. Before starting, stopping, or changing any medications while taking methyldopa, please get the advice of your doctor or pharmacist. the following nonprescription products may interact with methyldopa: iron supplements or vitamins containing iron. Be sure to let your doctor and pharmacist know that you are taking these medications before you start taking methyldopa. Do not start any of these medications while taking methyldopa without discussing with your healthcare provider.. tell you doctor if you have or have ever had kidney or liver disease, including hepatitis or cirrhosis. tell your doctor if you are pregnant, plan to become pregnant, or are breast-feeding. If you become pregnant while taking methyldopa, call your doctor. talk to your doctor about the risks of taking methyldopa if you are 65 years of age or older. Older adults should not usually take methyldopa because it is not as safe as other medications that can be used to treat the same condition. if you are having surgery, including dental surgery, tell the doctor or dentist that you are taking methyldopa. you should know that t
